description_long: This sculpture depicts two cherubic children entangled with a large serpent. The upper child stands upright, one arm raised above his head, while the other child is crouched below, both appearing to struggle with the coiling snake. The figures are rendered in a classical style, with detailed musculature and expressive faces. The surface of the sculpture shows signs of weathering and lichen growth, suggesting it has been exposed to the elements for some time. The entire composition rests on a rectangular plinth, providing stability and elevating the dramatic scene. The stone material gives the piece a sense of permanence and historical gravitas.
description: A weathered stone sculpture of two children wrestling with a large serpent, set on a rectangular plinth. The figures are detailed and expressive, with visible signs of age and outdoor exposure.
description_view_0: The front view shows both children and the serpent clearly, with the upper child standing and the lower child crouched, all faces and limbs visible.
description_view_1: The left side view emphasizes the profile of the upper child and the coiling serpent, with less visibility of the lower child's face.
description_view_2: The rear view highlights the backs of both children and the winding body of the serpent, with weathering and lichen more apparent.
description_view_3: The right side view shows the upper child's face in profile and the serpent's body wrapping around both figures, with the lower child partially obscured.
attribute_subject_matter: two children and a serpent in a dramatic struggle
attribute_location_context: No specific setting is visible; the sculpture appears suitable for outdoor installation, such as a park or garden.
